source: CT6-6 requirements §4 (MCP-1 … MCP-3): MCP design agents that standardize LLM output via explicit output contracts for agents embedded in an application — component 4 of the in-repo tier

scenarios: a closed contract validates conforming and rejects non-conforming values
acceptance_criteria: build_output_contract: closed JSON Schema + structured-output tool + enum type-checked at build

scenarios: the skill + agent state the pattern and the bound
acceptance_criteria: skills/mcp-output-contract-design/SKILL.md documents MCP-1…3 + the four-part pattern + embedded-in-app framing
agents/mcp-design-agent.md enumerates producer points + designs a contract each
agent bounded to .architect-team/mcp-design/; never writes the app's code
reject-not-produce distinction stated (forced-tool + retry is the app's runtime)
minimal-validator limits named (no format/pattern/range/nested-object); json_schema is standard JSON Schema
clean boundary vs verified-agent-output (VAO = CT6's own agents; this = the user's app)
JSON-type guards (bool-not-integer, int-is-number, no array-items crash, empty fields, enum-mismatch rejected, object no-recurse)
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only engine + a skill + an agent + tests + docs).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — provider-agnostic contract design; the engine + CLI are exercised by the pytest suite (incl. real subprocess round-trip).
testing_authorizations.both_layer_front_to_back: N/A — single-layer (Python engine + skill/agent doctrine) change.
